at_realistic_student_error_magnitude_fra.reading: all contractive-to-neutral at the student's actual error size

amplification.vs_glm: GLM amplified 1.4 to 2.4x at every stratum and every magnitude, worse at smaller; DeepSeek's worst case is 1.18 at L38 quarter-magnitude, and at realistic magnitudes it does not amplify at any stratum

correction: the two-stratum CONTRACTIVE claim was premature. The late stratum (L38) is THRESHOLD_STABLE with the GLM worse-at-smaller signature, so DeepSeek is NOT uniformly contractive. It is substantially more favourable than GLM, but the clean claim overreached and is corrected here.
favourable_facts: at the student's actual error magnitude, amplification is 0.82/0.96/0.99 across early/mid/late, contractive to neutral
the student fits the MoE FAR better at depth than GLM: skill 0.97-0.98 at L20/L38 versus GLM's 0.53, so errors are tiny to begin with
worst-case amplification 1.18 is mild next to GLM's 2.4
forward: validated streamed forward, official DeepseekV4DecoderLayer, real dequantized weights, one block resident at a time

functional_escape.input: real contextual pre-MoE hidden, not embedding-seeded
functional_escape.note: bounded: 224 positions, one layer, thin lower bound

honest_status: DeepSeek is materially more favourable than GLM: the functional escape exists, fits far better at depth, and does not amplify at realistic error magnitudes at any tested stratum. But it is NOT uniformly contractive; the deep layer shows the mild GLM worse-at-smaller signature. Whether the escape survives a full multi-layer cascade is open. This is a promising, bounded, corrected result, not a promotion and not a clean contraction law.
mechanism_hypothesis: the manifold-constrained hyper-connections (four streams mixed by a doubly-stochastic Sinkhorn matrix) are contractive by construction; a doubly-stochastic mix cannot increase the l2 of a deviation on average, which is the opposite of GLM's single-stream residual that amplified it
not_evidence_of: capability, a full-model result, or a promoted representation. Bounded tokens, two strata, modest student skill. This is a paradigm-direction result, not a servable model.
real_caveats: L38 is threshold-stable with worse-at-smaller behaviour, GLM's signature, so a full cascade could drive errors into the mildly-expansive small-magnitude regime
whether the escape composes in a student-in-every-layer cascade is genuinely OPEN and needs that measurement, not settled by single-layer perturbations
significance: the first cross-parent result that points AWAY from the GLM expansive law: the functional escape exists AND the residual stream does not amplify its error
superseded_by_cascade: DEEPSEEK_V4_FLASH_CASCADE_DECISION.json: the full cascade showed the functional student does NOT compose (router divergence at L4, final skill catastrophic). The favourable single-layer amplification did not imply composition. Final verdict FUNCTIONAL_PARTIAL_ONLY.
what_is_still_required_before_promotion: stronger existence statistics (more positions, tighter lower bound)
a multi-layer rollout with a student in every layer, not a single-layer perturbation
exact complete-model physical accounting at fp4-relative rates
what_this_reopens: a functional stream on DeepSeek is now scientifically plausible where it was refuted on GLM. The contraction-first pilot's promotion gate (block skill + rollout stability + cross-depth replication + physical accounting + runtime path) can proceed, because the propagation gate that killed GLM is passed here
